item.title: Rumor: Diablo 4 Could Be Coming to Another Platform Soon
item.description:
Diablo 4 's upcoming Lord of Hatred expansion has been rated for Nintendo Switch in Indonesia, sparking rumors of a new platform release in the near future. While nothing is confirmed at this point, a Diablo 4 Nintendo Switch port would open the game up to an entirely new audience, and with the expansion release set for April 28, 2026, it could be the perfect time to bring fresh players into the fold.

Diablo 4 's initial launch in 2023 came on PC, PlayStation 4, PlayStation 5, Xbox One, and Xbox Series X/S. Despite launching on previous-generation Microsoft and Sony consoles, Blizzard's action RPG skipped the Nintendo Switch entirely. However, the series has a history with the platform. In November 2018, Blizzard released Diablo 3: Eternal Collection on the Switch, and Diablo 2: Resurrected followed upon its 2021 launch.

Diablo 4: Lord of Hatred Rated For Nintendo Switch in Indonesia

Ahead of its April 28 launch, Diablo 4: Lord of Hatred has been rated by the Indonesia Game Rating System, the same ratings board behind the recent 007 First Light ending leak. The rating lists the expansion for PC, PlayStation 5, Xbox Series X/S, and Nintendo Switch, despite no port being announced at this point in time. This offers some hope to fans who have been waiting for the action RPG to finally make its way to the Nintendo platform that there is some light at the end of the tunnel.

It should be noted that this is far from confirmation that a Diablo 4 Nintendo Switch port is on the way. It's possible that these platforms are a placeholder for the rating, and should still be taken with a grain of salt. Notably, the rating does not list the PlayStation 4 and Xbox One, both of which Lord of Hatred will be released on, nor does it mention the Nintendo Switch 2. And given Nintendo's focus on bringing third-party titles to the newer Switch 2, it would be pretty surprising for any potential Diablo 4 handheld port to focus on the older console at this point.

There have been tons of rumors about Diablo 4 making its way to Nintendo platforms in the past. Former Diablo head Rod Fergusson said there was "an opportunity there" for the game to come to the Nintendo Switch 2 last year, indicating that Blizzard was open to the possibility. Despite this, there has been no official word on a port in a while, so gamers will just have to wait and see if anything comes to fruition.

Who’s That Character?

Identify the silhouettes before time runs out.

5.0 s
0 / 10

Results

0

High Score: 0

—

More Games
Who’s That Character? Identify the silhouettes before time runs out.
Easy (7.5s)Medium (5.0s)Hard (2.5s)Permadeath (2.5s)
5.0 s
0 / 10
Results
0
High Score: 0 —
More Games

Diablo 4 is in an extremely strong place at the moment. Recent seasons have been met with praise by players, as Blizzard seems to be nailing the seasonal mechanics time and time again, striking the right balance between fun and power creep. There's plenty to suggest that the features coming in Diablo 4: Lord of Hatred will set the game up for yet another successful period ahead. The expansion's launch on April 28 will be the biggest content drop since 2024's Vessel of Hatred expansion.

9 /10

Diablo 4

Follow
Followed
Action RPG
Hack and Slash
Systems
OpenCritic Reviews
Top Critic Avg: 88 /100 Critics Rec: 93%

Powered by

item.title: 10 Great Horror Games With No Sequel
item.description:
As Silent Hill f and Resident Evil Requiem proved, horror franchises can last forever, producing a conveyor belt of sequels that reinvent or repeat the wheel. While these releases tend to attract the most attention, standalone new horror games are also common, to the point that it is probably the most active genre in this regard. Seriously, Steam seems to receive another first-person horror game every other day, and some of them are brilliant and horrifying!

Greatness doesn't need a bigger and better sequel. In fact, some of the greatest horror games ever are one-and-done affairs that need nothing further, even if a follow-up would still be nice. From decorated classics to modern staples, let's take a look at a few nightmares that will probably never start recurring.

We are going to be a bit strict with the definition of horror, so we are going to try to avoid borderline cases like Prey and Bloodborne . Obviously, both of those are amazing and are 100% among the greatest-ever games with no sequels.

Doki Doki Literature Club!

The Definitive Meta-Horror Game Of All Time

Doki Doki Literature Club!
Released
September 22, 2017

WHERE TO PLAY

DIGITAL

Developer(s)
Team Salvato
Platform(s)
PC, iOS
Genre(s)
Visual Novel, Horror
Powered by

Recently, Doki Doki Literature Club! has made headlines due to being removed from the Google Play Store for inappropriate content. This decision has rightfully garnered widespread criticism as a censorship overreach, especially since it involves one of the most beloved horror visual novels that is otherwise available on basically every modern platform.

Nowadays, DDLC really doesn't need an introduction, and everyone reading this likely already knows what it is and where it goes. However, make no mistake about it, Team Salvato's free game was utterly unpredictable and effective during its early days. Even if somebody goes in knowing that this "cute" dating sim visual novel eventually goes in some very dark and fourth wall-breaking direction, that knowledge doesn't lessen the impact of that shift. Why? Well, DDLC is just fantastically written.

Doki Doki Literature Club Plus! adds new context, but it is just an expansion of the original rather than a sequel.

Ad Infinitum

A Battle With Inner Demons That Gets Really Intense

Ad Infinitum
Action
Adventure
Horror
Systems
Released
September 9, 2023

WHERE TO PLAY

DIGITAL
PHYSICAL

Platform(s)
PS5, PS4, Xbox One, PC
Genre(s)
Action, Adventure, Horror
Powered by

Ad Infinitum takes players into the troubled mind of a man broken by his experiences during the First World War. The monsters in the game represent his inner demons as he comes to terms with traumatic memories that have affected his entire life and family.

Ad Infinitum is a solid horror experience that dares to tread into difficult territory. While the game doesn’t add anything new to the genre, the narrative, atmosphere, and design of the game work well. Ad Infinitum is a self-contained solo story that will likely never see a sequel. However, horror fans should enjoy the inventive creature design and compelling backdrop.

Moons of Madness

Sci-fi Cosmic Horror Done Confidently

Moons of Madness
Adventure
Puzzle
Survival Horror
Systems
Released
October 22, 2019

WHERE TO PLAY

DIGITAL

Developer(s)
Rock Pocket Games, Dreamloop Games
Platform(s)
PC, PS4, Xbox One
Genre(s)
Adventure, Puzzle, Survival Horror
OpenCritic Rating
Fair
Powered by

While Moons of Madness may not be a groundbreaking release, its unique take on the survival horror genre makes it a worthy addition to any gamer's library. Moons of Madness takes Lovecraftian horror into a sci-fi setting. Players control Shane Newehart, an engineer working at a remote Martian facility.

The game offers a lot of atmosphere, tense horror moments, and engaging puzzles. This isn’t a game where players can go in guns blazing. Moons of Madness deals more with the psychological than the action. While the game is a standalone narrative and not a sequel, it also shares a universe with the Secret World MMO. However, a sequel seems unlikely anytime in the near future.

Blair Witch

A Horror Game That Is Arguably Scarier Than The Blair Witch Movies

Blair Witch
7 /10
Released
August 30, 2019

WHERE TO PLAY

DIGITAL

Developer(s)
Bloober Team
Platform(s)
PS4, Xbox One, Switch, PC, Amazon Luna
Genre(s)
Survival Horror
OpenCritic Rating
Fair
Powered by

Survival horror fans will enjoy this atmospheric video game based in the same universe as the found-footage movie classic The Blair Witch Project (1999). In the game, players control former police officer Ellis Lynch as he helps in the search for a missing boy. He soon discovers there is more to the woods than he initially thought.

In Blair Witch , players explore a semi-open world, using stealth to avoid enemies, and the character's dog to find clues and solve puzzles. The game has an eerie atmosphere, with the character primarily using his flashlight to ward off enemies. The game delves into the psychological state of Ellis Lynch, and the choices players make can affect the ending of the game. While Blair Witch is a part of an established film franchise, it is a standalone game that is unlikely to see a direct sequel.

World of Horror

A Horror Game That Combines Junji Ito With HP Lovecraft

World of Horror
Released
December 8, 2023

WHERE TO PLAY

DIGITAL

Developer(s)
panstasz
Platform(s)
PC, PS4, PS5, Switch
Genre(s)
RPG, Horror, Roguelite
OpenCritic Rating
Strong
Powered by

World of Horror takes the cosmic horror of H.P. Lovecraft and Junji Ito and creates a unique indie adventure horror game. In the game, players must solve five mysteries spread out through different playthroughs to defeat the old god threatening the town.

The game is played with turn-based combat and a text-based narrative. The 1-bit art style creates a bleak tone, while the comic-like design gives the feeling of an interactive manga. World of Horror could only come from the indie gaming scene and offers an immersive experience that will not be for everyone. Indie gaming fans will love this cosmic horror release that is unlike any other game.

The Quarry

Live Through A Proper Cinematic Horror Story

The Quarry
8 /10
Released
June 10, 2022

WHERE TO PLAY

DIGITAL
PHYSICAL

Developer(s)
Supermassive Games
Platform(s)
PC, PS4, PS5, Xbox One, Xbox Series X, Xbox Series S
OpenCritic Rating
Strong
Genre(s)
Survival Horror, Drama, Thriller
Powered by

From the developers of the Dark Pictures Anthology series and Until Dawn , The Quarry is a fun horror game that plays like an interactive movie. Players control a group of young adults who have just finished working at a summer camp in the woods and are waiting to be picked up.

They soon discover that a terrifying beast is hunting them one by one. Players control different characters throughout the game while making dialogue choices and beating quicktime events. Decisions matter, as the game has over a hundred different endings. Any of the characters can die due to the player's choices. The Quarry is a fun, immersive game with a great cast and tense and gory scenes that work well as a standalone game.

Darkwood

A Twisted Hidden Gem That Puts The Survival In Horror

Darkwood
7 /10
Released
August 18, 2017

WHERE TO PLAY

DIGITAL

Developer(s)
Acid Wizard Studio
Platform(s)
Xbox One, PS4, PC, Switch
Genre(s)
Survival Horror
OpenCritic Rating
Strong
Powered by

Darkwood is unlike most horror games, but it is a must-play experience for fans of the genre. This eerie journey is slow-paced and challenging. Enemies can kill the player easily, and nothing is explained. This is a game that doesn’t hold the player's hand.

In Darkwood , players control a man stuck in a mysterious and dangerous wood who must attempt to escape. Throughout the game, players must explore the region and build and collect items to survive while uncovering secrets that will allow the player to progress further. The game’s bleak atmosphere and terrifying sound design create a constant feeling of isolation and claustrophobia. Darkwood is a hidden gem that is unlikely ever to see a sequel, as its developers are on indefinite hiatus. However, for horror fans, the game is a must-play experience.

Visage

A Brilliant Haunted House Horror

Visage
Horror
Systems
Released
October 30, 2020
ESRB
m
Developer(s)
SadSquare Studio

Platform(s)
PC, PS4, Xbox One
Genre(s)
Horror
OpenCritic Rating
Strong
Powered by

Visage follows in the same vein as the classic first-person survival horror game P.T . The game revolves around a haunted house and is split into three chapters, with a final section concluding the game. Each chapter tells the tragic story of someone who lived in the house and how they met their grisly end.

A chilling atmosphere permeates every second of Visage , which is elevated by strange bangs, doors closing, and lights turning off on their own. Playing Visage gives a constant sense of uneasiness. Its three stories are unique and present tough challenges and puzzles. Visage is a fantastic horror game that fans of the genre are sure to love. While Visage is a cult favorite, there is no word or rumor about a possible sequel.

Soma

A Sci-fi Horror Modern Classic

SOMA
8 /10
Released
September 15, 2015

WHERE TO PLAY

DIGITAL

Developer(s)
Frictional Games
Platform(s)
PS4, Xbox One, PC
Genre(s)
Horror
OpenCritic Rating
Strong
Powered by

Made by the same developers as the classic Amnesia series, SOMA is a standalone sci-fi horror game that delves deep into the mind of its protagonist. The game follows a man who wakes up in the future, in a deep underwater facility with no memory of how he got there.

Subscribe to the newsletter for more standout horror picks

Get curated deep dives into standalone horror games by subscribing to the newsletter—recommendations, developer context, and under-the-radar gems explained so you can discover more unforgettable scares and smartly expand your horror library.
By subscribing, you agree to receive newsletter and marketing emails, and accept our Terms of Use and Privacy Policy. You can unsubscribe anytime.

SOMA has become a classic thanks to its unique setting, terrifying enemies, and psychological horror. The game deals with ideas of identity, humanity, and consciousness, and while these sound like complex ideas, the game is never pretentious. While Frictional Games are known for their horror releases, they have publicly stated they are moving away from scary games, which means a SOMA sequel is unlikely.

Eternal Darkness: Sanity’s Requiem

The Best M-Rated Nintendo Game, And One Of The Most Influential Horror Games Ever

Eternal Darkness: Sanity’s Requiem
Released
June 24, 2002

WHERE TO PLAY

PHYSICAL

Developer(s)
Silicon Knights
Platform(s)
GameCube
Genre(s)
Survival Horror
Powered by

Does a game need to be scary to be horror? And, does it need to be scary to be considered great horror? The answer will vary from person to person, but Eternal Darkness: Sanity’s Requiem certainly presents a strong argument that scares are not 100% necessary to craft a horror masterpiece. A GameCube exclusive and the first M-rated game published by Nintendo, Eternal Darkness was absurdly ambitious and ahead of the curve, all the while still feeling like it belongs to the genre's early 2000s scene.

Set across multiple eras, the premise revolves around a woman who is investigating her grandfather's murder, which leads her to a strange book. Each chapter transports players into the body of a new character from unique periods, all of whom are connected by an evil force they encountered. The story is complex and consistently great, but Eternal Darkness ' biggest claim to fame is its Sanity Meter. When players are spotted by enemies, they begin to grow more insane, a process that is amplified by meta tricks directed at the user rather than the characters. We get trippy stuff like rooms that have been turned upside down, or even flashes of body horror. Heck, you might even get a fake blue screen of death.

We will probably never get a new Eternal Darkness , especially since the developer closed down in 2014. At most, Nintendo might throw this classic on NSO, although it would need to first acknowledge its existence.

item.title: All Murder Mystery Codes in Fortnite
item.description:
Fortnite Murder Mystery by Good Gamers is one of the more popular and long-running Creative maps out there, with plenty of players jumping in to farm XP, grind for new rewards, and simply have fun. Those diving in may have noticed a secret code terminal in the lobby, and it is here where players can claim a bunch of free rewards. Here is every Murder Mystery code in Fortnite .

All Murder Mystery Codes in Fortnite

Murder Mystery features a range of different codes that players can make use of. Just like many other Creative maps in Fortnite , these codes are all one-time use and may also expire after a certain period of time. Therefore, be sure to use them as soon as possible.

Murder Mystery is also receiving new codes regularly, so be sure to return this article to stay up to date. For those looking to grab some instant rewards, here is a breakdown of all the codes in Fortnite Murder Mystery:

All Working Codes

535625

2,000 Coins

713813

15 Gems and TheBoyDilly's Badge

147824

15 Gems and Discord Badge

828085

15 Gems and OnionFishy's Badge

805897

15 Gems and Ellie's Badge

819980

15 Gems and Vexbolts's Badge

992447

15 Gems and Saloodz's Badge

692388

15 Gems and GG Street's Badge

All Expired Codes

567074

15 Gems and Exclusive Badge

485905

15 Gems and Exclusive Badge

How to Use Codes in Fortnite Murder Mystery

To use codes in Murder Mystery, you will need to be in the lobby. From there, head over to the Badges room , and to the right you will find a code terminal. Interact with the terminal, enter your desired code, and hit enter to redeem it.

For those who want to enter codes in bulk while a game is starting, you can continue doing so if a match begins while you are still in the terminal UI. This will work as long as you are not eliminated or exit the menu. Otherwise, you will need to re-enter your codes back in the lobby.

Remember, all codes in Fortnite Murder Mystery are single-use only and cannot be redeemed again for more Gems or Coins, even if you join a different server.

Murder Mystery Map Code

5253-8468-3364

Fortnite
Released
September 26, 2017

WHERE TO PLAY

DIGITAL
PHYSICAL

ESRB
T for Teen - Diverse Content: Discretion Advised, In-Game Purchases, Users Interact
Developer(s)
Epic Games
Genre(s)
Survival, Battle Royale, Shooter, Action, Adventure
Platform(s)
PlayStation 5, PlayStation 4, Xbox Series X, Xbox Series S, Xbox One, Nintendo Switch 2, Nintendo Switch, PC, Android, iOS
OpenCritic Rating
Mighty
Powered by

item.title: Crimson Desert: How to Complete Aera Outlaw Bounty
item.description:
Aera outlaw is one of the 12 bounties available to collect in Pailune after completing Chapter 7 of Crimson Desert . This masked outlaw can be found right near Valgrind Tomb, close to the city of Pailune , and can be delivered to the guards fast.

However, it seems that many players have encountered difficulties in finding Aera in Pailune, so this guide aims to help. Here's where Aera is in Crimson Desert if you can't find her through the in-game map marker.

How to Unlock and Capture Aera in Crimson Desert

  • Before starting to chase outlaw bounties in Pailune, players first need to complete Chapter 7 , fully liberating the entire region of all enemies and reuniting the Greymanes in their homeland. (This will also reward you with Oongka as a playable character).
  • By exploring the town of Pailune after liberating it, numerous outlaw bounty notices start to pop up on wooden poles in the town or near the main roads, including Aera's.
  • Taking Aera's notice and reading it will add the bounty quest to the journal, where players can also track the outlaw's map location.
  • Aera hides right next to the Valgrind Tomb's entrance, located directly north of the city of Pailune , pretty close to the city. She often has two to four companions at her side, although they won't be hostile and will flee as soon as players approach the suspicious group.
  • Many players report difficulties in locating Aera in Crimson Desert , as the outlaw is nowhere to be seen near the map marker for them, and reloading the game does not always help. The most apparent issue here is that players may have already frightened Aera away during early exploration, without having started her bounty.
  • If that's the case and you can't find Aera on the spot near the Valgrind Tomb, check the surrounding area, as shown in the red circle on the map image above. Most likely, Aera will be found roaming nearby — in our case, east of Valgrind Tomb, closer to the river . Observe your mini-map closely for an outlaw icon to appear.
  • Chase Aera and quickly tackle her (X/A), then tie her up and prepare to deliver her to justice at the Pailune jail, which is thankfully, pretty close by, without the need of a horse.
  • By turning Aera into the Pailune guards, players will get a reward of 9 silver, as well as some Greymane contribution points that you can spend in the contribution shop nearby to get some outfit items.

Aera is yet another outlaw that is wanted alive for her crimes, so try not to kill her; otherwise, you'll get only half of the silver reward.

In Pailune, more outlaws can be claimed during Chapter 8 and beyond, including such exotic bounties as Wolfe the boarman, hiding in the forest disguised as the upright (and quite talkative) wild boar. Check our guide for finding and capturing him as well.

Follow
Followed
Crimson Desert
Action
Adventure
Open-World
Exploration
Systems
OpenCritic Reviews
Top Critic Avg: 78 /100 Critics Rec: 73%
Released
March 19, 2026
ESRB
Mature 17+ / Blood, Drug Reference, Intense Violence, Strong Language
Developer(s)
Pearl Abyss
Publisher(s)
Pearl Abyss

WHERE TO PLAY

DIGITAL
PHYSICAL

Genre(s)

Powered by

item.title: Crimson Desert: How to Complete Wolfe Outlaw Bounty
item.description:
Despite his fearsome name, Wolfe, an outlaw in Crimson Desert , is actually disguised as another animal — a huge boar hiding in the Pailune forests in Silver Wolf Mountain .

Players can find his bounty notice soon after completing Chapter 7, alongside the other Pailune outlaws (12 in total). Finding and capturing Wolfe isn't too challenging in the game and involves no combat at all, but you will have to have a keen sense of tracking. Here's how to do it.

How to Unlock and Capture Wolfe in Crimson Desert

  • First things first, to unlock most of the side quests in Pailune, players have to progress the main story up to Chapter 8 , fully liberating the entire region from Lonely Jackals and Black Bears in the process.
  • At this point, players naturally unlock playable Oongka in Crimson Desert and can tackle most side quests, including bounties, as either him or Kliff.

If you're wondering what my Kliff wears here, it's the full Crowcaller set (also known as the Blackwing armor set). Here's how you can get all the pieces to look cool and menacing while chasing outlaws in Pailune.

  • As players explore the city of Pailune after liberating the region, most of the 12 outlaw notices can be found on the wooden poles in the area. At times, several different bounty notices appear right next to each other, so just pay attention to the purple icons on your mini-map.
  • When discovering the Wolfe bounty notice, make sure to examine it to add the quest to the journal and be able to track the outlaw down via the map.
  • Wolfe wears a full boar disguise, including a distinct cloak with a boar head. He can be found hiding in the forest right next to several boars in Silver Wolf Mountain, northwest of Pailune city (see the image above). There are a couple of Abyss Nexus fast travel points nearby.
  • Upon approaching Wolfe, he might attempt to flee, so pay attention to his icon on the mini-map and quickly chase him down, as his disguise can work pretty well in the wilderness. Chase and tackle him (X/A) to be able to hit him and tie him up.
  • Since players can't teleport while carrying a bounty, Wolfe has to be delivered to the Pailune jail on horseback. It's a relatively long ride (around 1300 m) , but fortunately, it's easy to avoid any potential enemies if you are following the direct road to the city.
  • Turn Wolfe in to the guard to receive a reward of 12 silver and some Greymane contribution points . The shop for spending those can be found right next to the jail, and there are several items available for purchase.

Like with most bounties in Crimson Desert , Wolfe needs to be delivered alive for a full reward. If you kill him and deliver only his body, you'll get only half of the promised money.

Dozens of bounty outlaws await the players in Crimson Desert's vast multi-region world, and you can find guides for many of them to make the process of capturing them easier. Some are even tied to other quests, like the Dante the Great Thief bounty — one of the latest to unlock in Hernand for players looking for the full completion of the starting region.

Follow
Followed
Crimson Desert
Action
Adventure
Open-World
Exploration
Systems
